author: gb9t
content: Phase C /loop iter 60: 2 Tier-3 fs/sysfs docs (kernfs underlying + kobject framework). fs/sysfs/kernfs.md (struct kernfs_node + root + open_file + ops layouts, active-path locking via per-node atomic active counter w/ kernfs_get_active+_put_active+_drain — cornerstone of safe attribute removal during in-progress reads, kernfs_create_dir_ns+_file_ns+_link, recursive kernfs_remove w/ drain, KERNFS_NS_TAG per-namespace lookup, per-fd kernfs_open_file w/ atomic_write_len, struct kernfs_root per-instance state w/ kernfs_rwsem; OWNS mandatory models/fs/kernfs_active_path.tla — 11 REQ / 11 AC, 0 open) + fs/sysfs/kobject.md (kobject framework: struct kobject+kobj_type+kset+sysfs_ops layouts, lifecycle kobject_init→add (sysfs dir+ADD uevent)→del (REMOVE uevent)→put (kref→release), kref atomic refcount, 8 KOBJ_ACTION constants ADD/REMOVE/CHANGE/MOVE/ONLINE/OFFLINE/BIND/UNBIND, kobject_uevent+uevent_env via NETLINK_KOBJECT_UEVENT broadcast for udev integration, struct kset_uevent_ops filter+name+uevent hooks, default_groups auto-create, namespace-aware kobject_ns — 12 REQ / 11 AC, 0 open). 10 upstream paths validated against baseline 27a26ccfd528. Total: 159 .design docs. fs/sysfs Tier-2 COMPLETE: all 4 children done (overview + sysfs-core + kernfs + kobject). Next: pivot to fs/ext4/00-overview.md (the most-deployed Linux filesystem) or kernel/cgroup/00-overview.md (cgroup-v2 — backbone of containers/systemd resource control).
